briefs.title: Burnaby Council Meeting: Key Decisions from March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, several decisions were made that could affect local business owners. These include contract awards for water metering technology and hired equipment services, rezoning applications moving forward for mixed-use and self-storage developments, and the adoption of updated fees, parking meter, and bylaw enforcement rules.
briefs.action_required: Check if any rezoning or bylaw changes near your business location affect your operations or property.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Parking Meter and EV Charging Meter Bylaw Amendment — final adoption takes effect
Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw Amendment — final adoption takes effect
Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw Amendment — final adoption takes effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Zoning Changes, Housing Plans, and New Bylaws at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including rezoning applications for new mixed-use and residential developments, contract awards for city services, and final adoption of bylaw amendments covering parking meters, fees, and bylaw enforcement. Several rezoning proposals were advanced to first and second reading — meaning they are still moving through the approval process and have not yet received final approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, watch for upcoming public hearings where you can share your input.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving liquor primary licence for Solo Karaoke in Burnaby
briefs.summary: At the March 10, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to recommend that the provincial Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) — the body that issues liquor licences in BC — approve a liquor primary licence for Solo Karaoke at 102–6462 Kingsway. A liquor primary licence allows a business to serve alcohol as its main activity. The recommendation follows an investigation into previous allegations about the business, which staff found did not block approval.
briefs.action_required: If you operate a nearby business or have concerns, contact the BC LCRB directly — the final licence decision rests with them.
briefs.industries: Liquor

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll Confirmation
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Parcel Tax Roll Review Panel has voted to confirm and authenticate the 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll — a list of properties and the sewer-related charges assigned to each. This decision is now set to go before full City Council for approval at the March 24, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Check your property's sewer parcel tax charge on the 2026 roll before Council approves it.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to consider water metering contract, modular housing pilot, and rezoning proposals
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, several key items were up for decision, including a $4.2 million contract for new water metering technology, a modular four-plex housing pilot project (sent back to staff for review), and multiple rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments across Burnaby. These decisions could affect property owners, developers, and businesses operating near the affected sites.
briefs.action_required: Watch for future Council meetings where referred and rezoning items will return for final decisions.

key_dates.label: Burnaby modular housing pilot project — staff review report due back to Council
REZ #24-23 Grand Promenade mixed-use rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled
REZ #25-09 6535 Kingsway mixed-use rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled
REZ #25-21 2757 Norland Avenue self-storage rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026–2030 Strategic Plan
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's (BHA) five-year Strategic Plan, which targets 1,000 new rental housing units started by 2030. Approving this plan is also a required step before the City can release $2,000,000 in Year 2 operating funding to the BHA.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 24 — this shapes Burnaby's rental housing supply through 2030.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BHA 2026–2030 Strategic Plan approval
BHA financial statements & 2026 budget — Burnaby Council agenda
BHA first project milestone — 50 unit starts deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby paying $36,250 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $36,250.57 payment for the City's 2026 membership in the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — a province-wide organization that gives local governments a shared voice on policy issues. The money is already set aside in Burnaby's 2026 operating budget, so no new spending would be required if Council approves.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on March 24, 2026 — no direct action required for most business owners.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $4.2M contract for Burnaby water metering technology
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $4.2 million contract (including taxes) to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water meters across the city. This is the first phase of Burnaby's Universal Water Metering Strategy — about 5,500 meters would go to meter-ready properties, and roughly 500 homes with secondary suites would also receive meters. For now, water bills would stay as flat annual charges, not usage-based.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council decision — water meters may be installed at your property soon.

key_dates.label: Burnaby water metering contract award council vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Technology

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.6M modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,626,050 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit, three-storey rental home at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The City would own the building and rent out the units, with $350,000 in outside funding from FortisBC and the National Research Council reducing the City's share to about $2.28 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council meeting decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing policy in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award public meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on awarding 19 hired equipment contracts worth $29.8M
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services — things like dump trucks, excavators, tree chippers, and hydro-vac units — to support city crews on construction, maintenance, and emergency work. The proposed contracts would run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2029, with a total estimated value of $29,825,000 (about $9.9M per year), and an option to renew for two more years.
briefs.action_required: If you run an equipment or contracting business, watch for future RFSOs — the next opportunity to bid on city work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby hired equipment service contracts proposed start date
Burnaby hired equipment service contracts proposed end date
Burnaby Council vote on hired equipment contracts — meeting date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewal with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving and concrete services for one more year, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2027. The proposed renewal is valued at an estimated $1,155,000 (taxes included), covering road repairs, storm sewer work, and watermain projects on an as-needed basis.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council meeting decision if you work in paving or road construction in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby asphalt paving contract renewal period begins — if approved
Burnaby Council vote on Blackrete Paving contract renewal

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Major Mixed-Use Rezoning Applications Advance in Burnaby
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to move several rezoning applications — including a large mixed-use tower project at Grand Promenade/Austin Road, a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway, and a 5-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue — to First and Second Reading at future Council meetings. These are early steps in the approval process; final decisions have not yet been made.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near these sites, monitor upcoming Council meetings for public hearing dates and final rezoning decisions.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Four High-Rise Towers with 1,574 Homes Up for Approval in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to allow staff to move forward with a rezoning application (a formal request to change how land can be used) for 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court in Burnaby. The proposal, submitted by Pinnacle International, would replace a previously approved three-tower plan with four high-rise buildings of up to 40 storeys, containing roughly 1,133 market strata units, 141 replacement rental units, 300 non-market affordable rental units, and ground-floor retail space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this could affect nearby commercial tenants, landlords, and construction businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Carrigan Court rezoning application public meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider 87-Storey Mixed-Use Tower with Hotel at Lougheed
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application (REZ #24-23) that would allow developer Pinnacle International to amend an already-approved project near Lougheed Town Centre. The updated plan proposes two residential towers of 77 and 87 storeys, a 171-suite hotel, restaurants, and 1,828 condo units — replacing much of the previously approved office space and adding more floors than the original design.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect nearby commercial leasing, hotel competition, and construction activity in the Lougheed area.

key_dates.label: REZ #24-23 Burnaby rezoning bylaw — Final Adoption deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby — Council to Consider Rezoning
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application for 6535 Kingsway to its first formal readings. The proposal, submitted by Intracorp, would allow a 12-storey building with 290 rental apartments and roughly 951 m² of ground-floor retail shops to replace the existing fast-food drive-through on the site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could change the retail and rental landscape along Kingsway.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-09 — Council vote to advance rezoning to First and Second Reading

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-Storey Public Storage Facility Up for Approval at 2757 Norland Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application (REZ #25-21) that would allow a five-storey self-storage facility — about 1,008 units — to be built at 2757 Norland Avenue in Burnaby. A proposed bylaw would change the property's zoning from general industrial to a custom 'Comprehensive Development' zone tailored to this project, and no public hearing is planned since the proposal aligns with Burnaby's Official Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — this rezoning must reach final approval by December 9, 2026 or it will be cancelled.

key_dates.label: REZ #25-21 Burnaby rezoning — final adoption deadline or application cancelled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Endorsing Burnaby's First Poverty Reduction Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy — a community-built plan with 84 recommended actions across areas like housing, employment, health, and cost of living. If endorsed, staff would work with community partners to build an implementation plan, and the Mayor would write to the provincial government asking for ongoing funding to help cities carry out local poverty reduction work.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — endorsed strategy could shape future funding, programs, and services affecting your business community.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Poverty Reduction Strategy endorsement

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Healthcare, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Consent Agenda: Building Permits, City Investments, and Burnaby 2050 Plan Updates
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Council meeting, four information items were approved as part of the Consent Agenda. These included building permit activity reports for January and February 2026, a year-end update on the City's 2025 investment program with a 2026 forecast, a status update on major civic construction projects, and an overview of how Burnaby plans to put its long-term community plan — Burnaby 2050 — into action.
briefs.action_required: Review the building permit activity reports if you are planning construction or development in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby building permit activity reports for January–February 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving two building permit activity reports covering January 1 to February 28, 2026. These reports give Council a snapshot of construction activity in Burnaby during those two months. No new rules or requirements are being proposed — this is an information item only.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby's 2025 Investment Results and 2026 Forecast
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on how Burnaby managed its $2.08 billion investment portfolio in 2025, earning $106.63 million at a 4.43% return. The report also forecasts a lower return of around 3.20% (roughly $71 million) for 2026, due to falling interest rates and a cautious, cash-focused strategy ahead of major capital spending.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — lower investment returns may affect city budgets and future service funding.
briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Status Update on Six Major Burnaby Civic Construction Projects
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on six major civic building projects across Burnaby, including a new recreation centre at Burnaby Lake, a new RCMP building, and expanded community centres at Cameron and Brentwood. The report — up for acceptance at the March 24, 2026 council meeting — confirms most projects are on schedule and on budget, with completion dates ranging from late 2026 to 2030.
briefs.action_required: Monitor road and traffic impacts near Joe Sakic Way, Sperling Ave, and Norland Ave if your business is nearby.

key_dates.label: Mintara Restaurant expansion handover to Parks, Recreation & Culture — Burnaby
James Cowan Theatre handover to Parks, Recreation & Culture — Burnaby
Community Safety Building (RCMP) construction completion — Burnaby
Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre handover to Parks, Recreation & Culture
Cameron Community Centre and Library handover to Parks, Recreation & Culture — Burnaby
Brentwood Community Centre construction completion — Burnaby

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan Launch
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a report on the proposed launch of an Implementation Action Plan (IAP) for the newly adopted Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) — a long-term blueprint that guides how the city grows and develops. The IAP would track progress, assign responsibilities, and report publicly on how well the city is meeting its planning goals, with a final plan expected to go before Council in early 2027.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby 2050 webpage for updates — this plan will shape future zoning and development rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan — Council review expected

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Information Reports — March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a set of information reports at its March 24, 2026 meeting. No body text or specific report details were provided, so the exact topics under consideration are not yet known. Business owners should check the full agenda on the City of Burnaby website for details that may affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby website for the full agenda before the March 24, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby City Council information reports meeting date

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Advances Zoning Changes and Adopts Parking, Fees & Bylaw Enforcement Updates
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council gave first and second reading — meaning early-stage approval, not final sign-off — to three zoning bylaw amendments affecting sites on Lougheed Hwy, Springer Avenue, and Imperial Street. Council also gave final adoption to three bylaws updating parking meter rules, city fees and charges, and bylaw notice enforcement, meaning those changes are now in effect.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business address falls near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, 2209–2225 Springer Ave, or 5058 Imperial St — rezoning could affect your area.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Three Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendments Up for First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Council meeting, three proposed zoning bylaw amendments — covering properties at 5058 Imperial Street, 6800 Lougheed Hwy, and 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue — were given first and second reading (early approval steps before a bylaw becomes law). These rezoning proposals still need further readings and final adoption before they take effect, and the one at 5058 Imperial Street faced notable opposition from both Council members and nearby residents.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near these sites, monitor upcoming Council meetings for the final adoption vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 20-storey mixed-use rental tower at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw (Bylaw No. 14802) that would allow a 20-storey mixed-use building at 5058 Imperial Street. The building would include market rental apartments and a double-height commercial space at street level, replacing a previously approved plan for a smaller six-storey rental building on the same site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and commercial leasing opportunities.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on rezoning and master plan update at 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the master plan — a high-level blueprint for how a site can be developed — for the Burnaby Lake Village area at 6800 Lougheed Hwy. The changes would revise the site layout, adjust building heights and massing (how big and bulky buildings can be), and add more open space, bringing the plan in line with Burnaby's new long-term planning rules (OCP 2050). No new construction or land subdivision is proposed at this stage.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect development plans or property use near 6800 Lougheed Hwy.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower with Retail at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a zoning bylaw amendment that would allow a 49-storey residential tower — including a 9-storey stepped base (podium) with retail shops facing the street — to be built at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue in Burnaby. If approved, the rezoning would change what is legally permitted on this land to match the proposed mixed-use development designed by FaulknerBrowns Architects.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — approval could bring new retail space and foot traffic to the Springer Avenue area.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Parking Meter, Fees, and Bylaw Enforcement Rules Up for Final Approval
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to give final approval to three bylaw amendments: one updating parking meter and EV charging meter rules, one changing the city's consolidated fees and charges, and one amending how bylaw notices are enforced. All three passed unanimously and are now adopted city law.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby fees and charges bylaw to check if any new costs apply to your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging meter rules and fee structures
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would update how public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations are defined and how drivers are charged for using them. The proposed changes would allow fees to be based on either time (how long you park) or energy used (how much electricity you draw), and would set new limits on how long a vehicle can occupy a public EV charging space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final adoption date — this could affect how you manage EV charging at your business location.

key_dates.label: Burnaby EV Charging Meter Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 — final adoption vote

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging fees at public charging stations
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would update the fees charged at Burnaby's public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The proposed bylaw would set new rates based on either energy used (per kWh) or time spent charging — for example, $0.2865/kWh at Level 2 stations and $0.3479/kWh at Level 3 stations, plus idle fees if a vehicle stays plugged in after charging is done.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business uses or offers public EV charging — these new rates could affect your costs or customer experience.

key_dates.label: Burnaby EV charging fees bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to add $75 fine for overstaying public EV charging spots
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would add a new fine for drivers who exceed the time limit at public electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in Burnaby. If approved, the penalty would be $75 (or $60 if paid early), targeting EV drivers who stay too long and block charging access for others.
briefs.action_required: If your business has or relies on public EV charging spots, watch for the council vote outcome.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Correspondence Items at March 24, 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a correspondence item submitted for their decision at the March 24, 2026 meeting. Additional correspondence has been published in a separate public package for that date, but details on the specific item requiring a council decision have not been disclosed in this agenda entry.
briefs.action_required: Check the full March 24, 2026 Council Correspondence package for details that may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing 20-Storey Development at 5058 Imperial Street, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a petition signed by 175 residents opposing a proposed 20-storey building at 5058 Imperial Street in Burnaby (rezoning application REZ #25-13). Residents say the development would block views, threaten retirement stability, and raise neighbourhood safety concerns — and they are asking Council not to move the rezoning forward at this time.
briefs.action_required: Attend the March 24, 2026 Council meeting to follow the discussion on this rezoning proposal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — REZ #25-13 petition considered

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Councillor Alison Gu announces she will not seek re-election in October 2026
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, Councillor Alison Gu announced she will not be running for re-election when her current term ends. She highlighted key work during her time on Council, including affordable housing initiatives, green space protections, cycling infrastructure, and climate policy. Her seat will be open in the October 2026 municipal election.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the October 2026 Burnaby municipal election — a Council seat will be open.

key_dates.label: Burnaby municipal election — Councillor Gu's seat open for candidates

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Recognize Joanne Morgan as Burnaby's 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has voted unanimously to approve Joanne Morgan as the 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year, with the Kushiro Cup to be presented at the City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026. This is a civic recognition award and does not affect business regulations or operations.

key_dates.label: Kushiro Cup presentation — Burnaby City Appreciation Dinner

briefs.industries: Professional Services
